etymology.description: semantic 灬 ("fire") + phonetic 昭 (zhāo, "clear, bright") = illuminate
etymology.interpretation: The character 照 (zhào) combines 灬, representing "fire," with 昭 (zhāo, "clear, bright") as the phonetic, conveying the idea of something being illuminated or shining clearly, paralleling the bright qualities of fire.

examples.chinese: 太阳照在海面上(太陽照在海面上)。
examples.english: The sun shines on the surface of the sea.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢照相(他喜歡照相)。
镜子可以照出你的形象(鏡子可以照出你的形象)。
examples.pinyin: Jìngzi kěyǐ zhào chū nǐ de xíngxiàng.
examples.english: The mirror can reflect your image.
